configurePresets.name: base
configurePresets.description: Shared base preset, inherited by the real ones. `hidden: true` excludes this preset from `cmake --list-presets` and rejects `cmake --preset base`, but still allows inheritance via `inherits` — exactly what you want for an incomplete template (no `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE` here). RESEARCH: (a) what schema `version` do you need — check the cmake-presets(7) version-to-feature table — and what breaks if you pick too low? (b) when does inheritance hurt readability vs help it? (c) why does this schema have BOTH `environment` and `cacheVariables` — what belongs in each?

configurePresets.name: debug
configurePresets.description: Default Debug build: -O0 -g, NDEBUG undefined (so assert() fires), no -DNDEBUG. All inherited from CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug toolchain defaults — see cmake-variables(7) for CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_DEBUG.

configurePresets.name: release
configurePresets.description: Default Release build: -O3 -DNDEBUG (so assert() is compiled out), no debug info. All inherited from CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release toolchain defaults — see cmake-variables(7) for CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_RELEASE.

buildPresets.name: debug
buildPresets.description: Builds in debug config, see debug config above

testPresets.name: debug
testPresets.description: Runs ctest against the debug build. Tests live in tests/ and are registered via doctest_discover_tests; doctest is pulled in via FetchContent. See CLAUDE.md for the framework rationale (compile speed for tight inner-loop iteration).

testPresets.name: release
testPresets.description: Runs ctest against the release build. Tests live in tests/ and are registered via doctest_discover_tests; doctest is pulled in via FetchContent. See CLAUDE.md for the framework rationale (compile speed for tight inner-loop iteration).

vendor._about_this_field: JSON doesn't support comments, so this object exists only as a scratchpad for research prompts. CMake should ignore unknown top-level keys; if it warns, move these notes into per-preset `description` fields and delete this block.
vendor._ccache: TODO: wire ccache in. Two common places: (1) here in cacheVariables on `base` — set CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER to "ccache". (2) inside CMakeLists.txt with find_program(CCACHE_PROGRAM ccache) + set(CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER ...). Read both approaches. Question to answer: what happens when both are set, and which scope do you actually want for a project that other people might clone?
vendor._clang_tidy: TODO: decide whether clang-tidy runs automatically on every build (CMAKE_CXX_CLANG_TIDY) or only on demand (separate CI step / pre-commit). The first is more thorough but slower and noisier; the second is easier to ignore. Pick one consciously.
vendor._sanitizers: TODO: add a 'asan' or 'debug-asan' configurePreset that inherits debug and turns on -fsanitize=address,undefined. Research: which sanitizers are mutually exclusive? Which need extra runtime flags (e.g. ASAN_OPTIONS)? Which ones does Apple's Clang on macOS not ship?
vendor._user_presets: TODO: read about CMakeUserPresets.json. When should something live there instead of in this file? (Hint: think about what other people on your machine vs other people on other machines should see.)
